description: Nalaka Indrajith Udugampola Vs Chairman of the Administrative Appeals Tribunal and Others Nalaka Indrajith Udugampola Vs Chairman of the Administrative Appeals Tribunal and Others View More before Ho.n.Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Nalaka Indrajith Udugampola Vs Chairman of the Administrative Appeals Tribunal and Others Nalaka Indrajith Udugampola Vs Chairman of the Administrative Appeals Tribunal and Others View More
judgement_by: Ho.n.Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner was involved in two robberies while serving as a police officer. Though he was acquitted from criminal proceedings, he was charge-sheeted by a disciplinary panel appointed by the Senior DIG of the range to which he was attached. Panel of Inquiry found him guilty on the strong evidence against him; findings against him by the Panel of Inquiry was further affirmed by the Public Service Commission and Administrative Appeal Tribunal. However, on sympathetic grounds he was permitted to retire. The Petitioner challenges the vires of the appointment of Panel of Inquiry and its findings. Held – the Petitioner, being a Sub- Inspector, is subject to the disciplinary control of the Senior DIG of the range to which he is attached, therefore, the appointment is intra vires and based on the facts, the final findings of the AAT is justified; thus, Application dismissed.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
